'京都府木津川市木津大谷105', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Okadakuni-jinja}} [[JA:岡田国神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11473495}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2016-10}} {{神社 | 名称=Okadakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}{{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352}}Kizu Ōtani 105 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960}} | 神事=Kizu Mikoshi Taiko Festival (futon taiko-float festival) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okadakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okadakuni-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Okadakuni Shrine''' (おかだくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kizu Ōtani, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major shikinaisha), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deities === Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} are enshrined as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Okinagatarashi-nakahiko-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Tennō}}) enshrined additionally. The original deity of Okadakuni Shrine was Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto. Sugawara no Michizane has been enshrined since the Heian period, and until the Edo period this shrine was called Tenjingū or Kizu-eki Sōsha Tenjinsha. === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in the ninth month of {{ill|lt=Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092}}), when Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto was enshrined. The first appearance in official history is an entry in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}} for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), recording the grant of the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} to this shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as a major shrine. In the Heian period, as {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725}} spread, Sugawara no Michizane (Tenjin) was jointly enshrined here, and from then on the shrine came to be called Tenjingū. In {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340}} 1 (938), in the 11th month, the co-enshrined Hachimangū was founded. In May of Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. In March of Meiji 11 ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776}}), the government officially identified this shrine as the shikinaisha Okadakuni Shrine, and it was renamed Okadakuni Shrine. Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1983 (Shōwa 58), part of the 40,000-tsubo precinct land was sold to the Japan Housing Corporation, and the proceeds were used to build new shrine buildings. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたくにしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|大おかたくにしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたくにしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|大おかたくにしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

名称=Okadakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}Kizu Ōtani 105 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}} | 神事=Kizu Mikoshi Taiko Festival (futon taiko-float festival) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okadakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okadakuni-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Okadakuni Shrine''' (おかだくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kizu Ōtani, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shikinaisha), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} are enshrined as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Okinagatarashi-nakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) enshrined additionally. The original deity of Okadakuni Shrine was Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto. Sugawara no Michizane has been enshrined since the Heian period, and until the Edo period this shrine was called Tenjingū or Kizu-eki Sōsha Tenjinsha. === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in the ninth month of {{ill|lt=Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}), when Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto was enshrined. The first appearance in official history is an entry in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), recording the grant of the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to this shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a major shrine. In the Heian period, as {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, Sugawara no Michizane (Tenjin) was jointly enshrined here, and from then on the shrine came to be called Tenjingū. In {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 1 (938), in the 11th month, the co-enshrined Hachimangū was founded. In May of Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. In March of Meiji 11 ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), the government officially identified this shrine as the shikinaisha Okadakuni Shrine, and it was renamed Okadakuni Shrine. Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1983 (Shōwa 58), part of the 40,000-tsubo precinct land was sold to the Japan Housing Corporation, and the proceeds were used to build new shrine buildings. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたくにしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|大おかたくにしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shikinaisha), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} are enshrined as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Okinagatarashi-nakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) enshrined additionally. The original deity of Okadakuni Shrine was Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto. Sugawara no Michizane has been enshrined since the Heian period, and until the Edo period this shrine was called Tenjingū or Kizu-eki Sōsha Tenjinsha. === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in the ninth month of {{ill|lt=Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}), when Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto was enshrined. The first appearance in official history is an entry in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), recording the grant of the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to this shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a major shrine. In the Heian period, as {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, Sugawara no Michizane (Tenjin) was jointly enshrined here, and from then on the shrine came to be called Tenjingū. In {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 1 (938), in the 11th month, the co-enshrined Hachimangū was founded. In May of Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. In March of Meiji 11 ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), the government officially identified this shrine as the shikinaisha Okadakuni Shrine, and it was renamed Okadakuni Shrine. Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1983 (Shōwa 58), part of the 40,000-tsubo precinct land was sold to the Japan Housing Corporation, and the proceeds were used to build new shrine buildings. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたくにしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|大おかたくにしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}} | 神事=Kizu Mikoshi Taiko Festival (futon taiko-float festival) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okadakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okadakuni-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Okadakuni Shrine''' (おかだくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kizu Ōtani, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shikinaisha), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} are enshrined as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Okinagatarashi-nakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) enshrined additionally. The original deity of Okadakuni Shrine was Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto. The first appearance in official history is an entry in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), recording the grant of the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to this shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a major shrine. In the Heian period, as {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, Sugawara no Michizane (Tenjin) was jointly enshrined here, and from then on the shrine came to be called Tenjingū. In {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 1 (938), in the 11th month, the co-enshrined Hachimangū was founded. In May of Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. In March of Meiji 11 ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), the government officially identified this shrine as the shikinaisha Okadakuni Shrine, and it was renamed Okadakuni Shrine. Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1983 (Shōwa 58), part of the 40,000-tsubo precinct land was sold to the Japan Housing Corporation, and the proceeds were used to build new shrine buildings. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたくにしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|大おかたくにしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}Kizu Ōtani 105 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}} | 神事=Kizu Mikoshi Taiko Festival (futon taiko-float festival) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okadakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okadakuni-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Okadakuni Shrine''' (おかだくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kizu Ōtani, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shikinaisha), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} are enshrined as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Okinagatarashi-nakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) enshrined additionally. The original deity of Okadakuni Shrine was Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto. Sugawara no Michizane has been enshrined since the Heian period, and until the Edo period this shrine was called Tenjingū or Kizu-eki Sōsha Tenjinsha. === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in the ninth month of {{ill|lt=Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}), when Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto was enshrined. The first appearance in official history is an entry in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), recording the grant of the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to this shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a major shrine. In the Heian period, as {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, Sugawara no Michizane (Tenjin) was jointly enshrined here, and from then on the shrine came to be called Tenjingū. In {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 1 (938), in the 11th month, the co-enshrined Hachimangū was founded. In May of Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. In March of Meiji 11 ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), the government officially identified this shrine as the shikinaisha Okadakuni Shrine, and it was renamed Okadakuni Shrine. Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1983 (Shōwa 58), part of the 40,000-tsubo precinct land was sold to the Japan Housing Corporation, and the proceeds were used to build new shrine buildings. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide {{DEFAULTSORT:おかたくにしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|大おかたくにしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

名称=Okadakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}Kizu Ōtani 105 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}} | 神事=Kizu Mikoshi Taiko Festival (futon taiko-float festival) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okadakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okadakuni-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Okadakuni Shrine''' (おかだくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kizu Ōtani, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shikinaisha), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} are enshrined as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Okinagatarashi-nakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) enshrined additionally. The original deity of Okadakuni Shrine was Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto. Sugawara no Michizane has been enshrined since the Heian period, and until the Edo period this shrine was called Tenjingū or Kizu-eki Sōsha Tenjinsha. === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in the ninth month of {{ill|lt=Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}), when Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto was enshrined. The first appearance in official history is an entry in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), recording the grant of the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to this shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a major shrine. In the Heian period, as {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, Sugawara no Michizane (Tenjin) was jointly enshrined here, and from then on the shrine came to be called Tenjingū. In {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 1 (938), in the 11th month, the co-enshrined Hachimangū was founded. In May of Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. In March of Meiji 11 ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), the government officially identified this shrine as the shikinaisha Okadakuni Shrine, and it was renamed Okadakuni Shrine. Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1983 (Shōwa 58), part of the 40,000-tsubo precinct land was sold to the Japan Housing Corporation, and the proceeds were used to build new shrine buildings. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|大おかたくにしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '京都府木津川市木津大谷105', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Okadakuni-jinja}} [[JA:岡田国神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11473495}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2016-10}} {{神社 | 名称=Okadakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}Kizu Ōtani 105 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}} | 神事=Kizu Mikoshi Taiko Festival (futon taiko-float festival) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okadakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okadakuni-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Okadakuni Shrine''' (おかだくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kizu Ōtani, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shikinaisha), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} are enshrined as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Okinagatarashi-nakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) enshrined additionally. The original deity of Okadakuni Shrine was Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto. Sugawara no Michizane has been enshrined since the Heian period, and until the Edo period this shrine was called Tenjingū or Kizu-eki Sōsha Tenjinsha. === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in the ninth month of {{ill|lt=Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}), when Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto was enshrined. The first appearance in official history is an entry in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), recording the grant of the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to this shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a major shrine. In the Heian period, as {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, Sugawara no Michizane (Tenjin) was jointly enshrined here, and from then on the shrine came to be called Tenjingū. In {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 1 (938), in the 11th month, the co-enshrined Hachimangū was founded. In May of Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. In March of Meiji 11 ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), the government officially identified this shrine as the shikinaisha Okadakuni Shrine, and it was renamed Okadakuni Shrine. Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1983 (Shōwa 58), part of the 40,000-tsubo precinct land was sold to the Japan Housing Corporation, and the proceeds were used to build new shrine buildings. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

名称=Okadakuni Shrine | 画像=[[File:Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}Kizu Ōtani 105 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}} | 神事=Kizu Mikoshi Taiko Festival (futon taiko-float festival) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okadakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okadakuni-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Okadakuni Shrine''' (おかだくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kizu Ōtani, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shikinaisha), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} are enshrined as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Okinagatarashi-nakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) enshrined additionally. The original deity of Okadakuni Shrine was Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto. Sugawara no Michizane has been enshrined since the Heian period, and until the Edo period this shrine was called Tenjingū or Kizu-eki Sōsha Tenjinsha. === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in the ninth month of {{ill|lt=Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}), when Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto was enshrined. The first appearance in official history is an entry in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), recording the grant of the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to this shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a major shrine. In the Heian period, as {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, Sugawara no Michizane (Tenjin) was jointly enshrined here, and from then on the shrine came to be called Tenjingū. In {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 1 (938), in the 11th month, the co-enshrined Hachimangū was founded. In May of Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. In March of Meiji 11 ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), the government officially identified this shrine as the shikinaisha Okadakuni Shrine, and it was renamed Okadakuni Shrine. Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1983 (Shōwa 58), part of the 40,000-tsubo precinct land was sold to the Japan Housing Corporation, and the proceeds were used to build new shrine buildings. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kizugawa, Kyoto]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shikinaisha), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} are enshrined as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Okinagatarashi-nakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) enshrined additionally. The original deity of Okadakuni Shrine was Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto. Sugawara no Michizane has been enshrined since the Heian period, and until the Edo period this shrine was called Tenjingū or Kizu-eki Sōsha Tenjinsha. === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in the ninth month of {{ill|lt=Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}), when Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto was enshrined. The first appearance in official history is an entry in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), recording the grant of the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to this shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a major shrine. In the Heian period, as {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, Sugawara no Michizane (Tenjin) was jointly enshrined here, and from then on the shrine came to be called Tenjingū. In {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 1 (938), in the 11th month, the co-enshrined Hachimangū was founded. In May of Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. In March of Meiji 11 ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), the government officially identified this shrine as the shikinaisha Okadakuni Shrine, and it was renamed Okadakuni Shrine. Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1983 (Shōwa 58), part of the 40,000-tsubo precinct land was sold to the Japan Housing Corporation, and the proceeds were used to build new shrine buildings. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kizugawa, Kyoto]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}} | 神事=Kizu Mikoshi Taiko Festival (futon taiko-float festival) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okadakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okadakuni-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Okadakuni Shrine''' (おかだくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kizu Ōtani, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shikinaisha), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} are enshrined as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Okinagatarashi-nakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) enshrined additionally. The first appearance in official history is an entry in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), recording the grant of the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to this shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a major shrine. In the Heian period, as {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, Sugawara no Michizane (Tenjin) was jointly enshrined here, and from then on the shrine came to be called Tenjingū. In {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 1 (938), in the 11th month, the co-enshrined Hachimangū was founded. In May of Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. In March of Meiji 11 ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), the government officially identified this shrine as the shikinaisha Okadakuni Shrine, and it was renamed Okadakuni Shrine. Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1983 (Shōwa 58), part of the 40,000-tsubo precinct land was sold to the Japan Housing Corporation, and the proceeds were used to build new shrine buildings. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kizugawa, Kyoto]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]]

{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建={{ill|lt=Empress Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}) | 本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}} | 神事=Kizu Mikoshi Taiko Festival (futon taiko-float festival) | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okadakuni Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Okadakuni-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Okadakuni Shrine''' (おかだくにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kizu Ōtani, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shikinaisha), and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} are enshrined as principal deities, with {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Okinagatarashi-nakahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) enshrined additionally. The original deity of Okadakuni Shrine was Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto. Sugawara no Michizane has been enshrined since the Heian period, and until the Edo period this shrine was called Tenjingū or Kizu-eki Sōsha Tenjinsha. === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in the ninth month of {{ill|lt=Saimei|Q235296|WD=Q235296|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 5 ({{ill|lt=659|Q31092|WD=Q31092|1=659}}), when Ikukunitama-no-Mikoto was enshrined. The first appearance in official history is an entry in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), recording the grant of the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to this shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} it is listed as a major shrine. In the Heian period, as {{ill|lt=Tenjin worship|Q66890725|WD=Q66890725|1=Tenjin faith}} spread, Sugawara no Michizane (Tenjin) was jointly enshrined here, and from then on the shrine came to be called Tenjingū. In {{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 1 (938), in the 11th month, the co-enshrined Hachimangū was founded. In May of Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a gōsha. In March of Meiji 11 ({{ill|lt=1878|Q7776|WD=Q7776|1=1878}}), the government officially identified this shrine as the shikinaisha Okadakuni Shrine, and it was renamed Okadakuni Shrine. Regarding the identity of Okadakuni Shrine, Kasuga Shrine, a sub-shrine in the precincts of Katsute Shrine (Ōno, Kamo-chō, Kizugawa City), is also treated as a candidate shrine. In 1983 (Shōwa 58), part of the 40,000-tsubo precinct land was sold to the Japan Housing Corporation, and the proceeds were used to build new shrine buildings. In 1988 (Shōwa 63), the old shrine building set was registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. === Precincts === Okadakuni-jinja, keidai.jpg|Shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Okadakuni-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|Haiden Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-kita.jpg|North parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja, tsumesho-minami.jpg|South parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidai.jpg|Old shrine buildings Okadakuni-jinja (old), honden.jpg|Old honden Okadakuni-jinja (old), haiden.jpg|Old haiden Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-kita.jpg|Old north parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), tsumesho-minami.jpg|Old south parishioners' office Okadakuni-jinja (old), buden.jpg|Old dance stage Okadakuni-jinja (old), keidaisha.jpg|Old precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine, 6 buildings (architecture) - registered April 15, 1988 (Shōwa 63). *** 2 honden buildings - Edo period. *** 1 haiden building - Edo period. *** 1 stage building - Edo period. *** 2 south/north parishioners' offices - Meiji period. ** Okadakuni Shrine documents, 76 items (plus one document box) (old documents) - owned by a private individual. Designated March 29, 2024 (Reiwa 6){{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/kouhou/documents/kouho_498_0.pdf Kyoto Prefectural Gazette, March 29, 2024]}} Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 2.. === Kyoto Prefecture Provisional Registered Cultural Properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Okadakuni Shrine Sessha Ebisu Shrine honden (architecture) - registered September 29, 2017 (Heisei 29). ** Okadakuni Shrine Daihannyakyō, 514 items (plus 6 scripture chests) (old documents) - registered March 24, 2023 (Reiwa 5). === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Kizu Station * Community bus: get off at Kawarada stop, about 8 minutes on foot * Parking available before the overpass on the approach road === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Okadakuni-jinja}} * [http://0774.or.jp/temple/okadakuni.html Okadakuni Shrine] - Kizugawa City Tourism Guide == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okadakuni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kizugawa, Kyoto]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]]
